judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with essentially equivalent evidence and rigor; they are comparable in quality.

report_1.evidence: `app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` returning an empty list, followed by the comment `# TODO: pdf export`. There is no export endpoint anywhere in the project... `app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." ... At most the document could say 2FA is *available* and opt-in.

report_2.evidence: `clinic-portal/app/routers/visits.py` contains only a stub `GET /visits` returning an empty list and the comment `# TODO: pdf export` (line 11)... This entire section must be removed. ... `clinic-portal/app/security/config.py` sets `TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False` with the comment "Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic." The claim states the opposite of what the code does. Correct it to "opt-in" or remove it.

report_1.evidence: `docs/release/captures.json` records that `after_scheduling.png` was captured from route `/billing/invoices` (versus `/schedule` for the "before"), and viewing the image confirms it — `before_scheduling.png` renders a screen titled "SCHEDULING CALENDAR" while `after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES".

report_2.evidence: `img/before_scheduling.png` renders "SCHEDULING CALENDAR", but `img/after_scheduling.png` renders "BILLING / INVOICES". The capture manifest confirms this was a capture-definition error... `captures.json` maps `before_scheduling.png` to route `/schedule` but `after_scheduling.png` to route `/billing/invoices`.

report_1.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **No** | Missing file; the email would ship a broken image. `captures.json` confirms: "expected capture; no corresponding file". ... `img/unused_capture_07.png` | Orphan file rendering "PATIENT LIST". Unused by the document

report_2.evidence: `img/after_export.png` | **no** | missing; `captures.json` explicitly says "expected capture; no corresponding file"... `img/unused_capture_07.png` | orphan file showing "Patient List"; referenced nowhere in the doc or the manifest

report_1.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33).** This is an internal drafting note, not customer content... Remove it

report_2.evidence: **The footer "Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance" (line 33).** This is internal drafting/attribution metadata, not customer content... a footer calling the document a "draft" definitely cannot go out in a customer email.
